block.engineers_decor_reforged.industria:
A small device that can be
 attached to a block to indicate
 if that block is powered. If the
 block can provide power itself,
 it uses the output of the block,
 otherwise it looks up the power
 that the block receives from
 adjacent blocks. Left click with
 dye to change the LED color.
 Blinks with a frequency of about 0.5Hz.

An industrial floor contact mat
 that emits a redstone signal when
 exposed to mechanical pressure
 on the top side (e.g. someone
 standing on it), and a short time
 after the pressure was released.
 Outputs are to the front and bottom.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).

A small wall mounted device that
 emits a redstone signal when it
 senses a high electric potential
 in the air, i.e. in thunder
 storms.§r The buttons on the front
 face configure the
 output signal strength. Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
item.engineers_decor_reforged.switchlink:
§6Erhält man, indem mit einer Ender-Perle ein Schalter mit der linken Maustaste angeklickt wird.§r
Die Perle merkt sich die Position (und Schalter-Typ), so dass der Schalter mit der Link-Perle ferngesteuert werden kann (durch Aktivieren der Perle in der InteractionHand), und so zu einem sog. "Link-Target" wird. Die Link-Perle kann auch mit Linksklick in andere Schalter eingesetzt werden (ebenfalls mit Linksklick), diese werden damit zu "Link-Sources". Die Eigenschaften von Link-Perlen (unabhängig von Schaltereinstellungen) können durch mehrfaches Linksklicken auf den Zielschalter geändert werden (rotiert durch die möglichen Optionen). Es können auch mehrere Perlen in einem Schalter untergebracht werden. Link-Perlen werden sich beschweren, wenn sie zu stark beansprucht sind, ihren Zielschalter nicht finden können, oder der Schalter ungeeignet ist (also genau hinhören). Unterschiedliche Schaltertypen reagieren verschieden auf Links - einfach experimentieren.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_lin:
A small glass device that observes
 in one direction and emits a
 redstone signal if it senses an
 entity. The observed volume is
 'range' blocks in front, and half
 a block to the sides. The range
 can be configured with small
 buttons on the front face, together
 with the output strength, the
 number of entities seen, and the
 entity type seen (mobs, players,
 animals, etc). Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on. Does not emit switching
 sounds. Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.rustic_le:
A switch that is manually flipped
 on or off like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.industria:
A switch that is manually flipped
 on or off like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).

A small device that can be
 attached to a block to indicate
 if that block is powered. If the
 block can provide power itself,
 it uses the output of the block,
 otherwise it looks up the power
 that the block receives from
 adjacent blocks. Left click with
 dye to change the LED color.

A compact sensor switch that
 checks the block it is mounted on.
 If the attached block has
 comparator output, inventory
 contents, or redstone power, the
 switch turns on and emits
 full-strength redstone.
 Mount it on storage, tanks, or
 redstone hardware to convert the
 attached signal into a simple
 on/off control line.

A display device that can be
 attached to a solid block to
 visualize the redstone power of
 that block. If the block can
 provide power itself, the gauge
 uses the output of it, otherwise
 the gauge will indirectly measure
 the power that the block receives
 from adjacent blocks. Strong and
 weak power is supported. Indirect
 weak measurements may have a
 short latency.
rsgauges.switchlinking.relayconfig.confv: Aktiviert wie der Ausgang des Quellschalters (invertiert)
rsgauges.switchlinking.target_assign.err: Dieser Schalter kann nicht durch Links aktiviert werden
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_rot:
A switch that is manually flipped
 on or off like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with any dye to color
 tint the switch.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.arrow_tar:
A target that emits a redstone
 pulse to the block it is attached
 to when it is hit with an arrow
 or activated by hand.
 Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
rsgauges.switchlinking.source_assign.err: Zielschalter ist zu weit weg, das würde nicht klappen
block.engineers_decor_reforged.rustic_sm:
A switch that outputs a redstone
 signal for a short time, like a
 button.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Activate while already active to
 extend the pulse time.
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
rsgauges.switch.feature.typepulse.help: §6Ein Taster, ähnlich wie ein normaler Minecraft Knopf.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_int:
Emits a signal with a defined on-
 and off-time. Configured using
 buttons on the front:
 - §1Blue: On-time§r
- §6Yellow: Off-time§r
- §2Green: Signal ramp up/down§r
- §4Red: Output power§r
 The timer is placed in standby
 mode for setup. Click on the front
 face to set it into run mode. Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
 As Switch Link target the timer
 toggles between run and standby
 mode.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.rustic_sh:
A metal plate emitting a redstone
 pulse when exposed to mechanical
 shock from above (e.g. something
 falls on it). Outputs are to the
 front and bottom. Power is always 15.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.industria:
An industrial manual redstone
 power dimmer. Allows to manually
 set an output signal between 0
 and 15 at the back side of the
 device. The vertical click position
 defines the signal strength.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).

block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_day:
A small glass device that emits a
 redstone signal from a configured
 start (day) time to a configured
 end time. The time span is set
 with buttons on the device front
 face (24 hour format, where 06:00
 is sunrise). These buttons also set
 output power and a
 randomizing value. The latter setting
 delays switching on or off for
 a random time span, where higher
 values cause longer delays. Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on. Does not emit switching
 sounds.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.industria:

Activate while already active to
 extend the pulse time.
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).

Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles

 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).

A linked receiver for control
 panels. Click with an Ender Pearl
 to create a linked pearl; triggering
 the pearl emits a full redstone
 signal at the receiver.

Emits a redstone signal if one or
 more blocks in front of it match a
 configured category. Configure
 with the buttons at the back side:
 - §1Range§r (observing how far)
 - §6Threshold§r (how many matches)
 - §3Debouncing§r (anti-jitter delay)
 §4- Output power§r
 - §2Filter§r (solid, fluid, wood log)
 Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).

block.engineers_decor_reforged.industria:
A small wall mounted device that
 emits a redstone signal when it
 rains on it. The buttons on the
 front face configure
 the output signal strength.
 Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this

§6Ein Anzeigegerät, das an einem Block befestigt werden kann um die Redstone-Signalstärke in diesem Block anzuzeigen.§r
Wenn der Block selbst Redstone-Ausgänge hat, dann wird die Signalstärke von diesem Block verwendet. Andernfalls wird die größte Signalstärke der Nachbarblöcke herangezogen (indirekte Messung). Starke und schwache Signale werden erkannt, indirekte Messungen von schwachen Signalen können aber eine kleine Anzeige-Verzögerung haben.

A small device that measures the
 local light level at its location
 and emits a redstone signal
 based on configured on/off
 thresholds set with small
 buttons on the front face.§r These buttons also set
 output power and debouncing. Debouncing
 delays switching off or on until
 the light level is stable, higher
 values mean better noise
 filtering, but also more switching
 delay. If on and off settings are
 identical, then the measured
 light level must exactly match
 the set value. Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.valve_whe:

 when exposed to mechanical
 pressure on the top (e.g. someone
 standing on it), and a short time
 after the pressure was released.
 Outputs are to the front and
 bottom. Does not emit any sounds.
 Click on the touch config up/down
 buttons to set output power,
 entity filter, entity count,
 and sensitivity.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Left-click with any dye to color
 tint the switch.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.industria:
§6Eine kleine Anzeige, die an einem Block angebracht werden kann, um anzuzeigen ob an diesem Block ein Redstone-Signal anliegt.§r
Wenn der Block selbst Redstone-Ausgänge hat, dann wird das Signal von diesem Block angezeigt. Ansonsten misst die Anzeige indirekt, ob an dem Block (von seinen Nachbarn) her ein Signal ansteht. Linksklick mit Farbe zum Einfärben.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.oldfancy_:
A switch that is manually flipped
 on or off like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
rsgauges.switch.feature.outputconfig.hel: Klick mit einer Redstonefackel (oder Links-Doppelklick mit leerer InteractionHand) rotiert die Ausgangskonfiguration (schwach, invertiert, usw).
rsgauges.switchlinking.source_assign.err: Dieser Schalter kann keinen Link aktivieren
block.engineers_decor_reforged.red_power:
Artificial red flower that
 looks very similar to a poppy,
 emitting a redstone signal to the
 front and bottom when something
 touches it (like a contact plate).
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.industria:
A switch that is manually flipped
 on or off like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).

A small device that can be
 attached to a block to indicate
 if that block is powered. If the
 block can provide power itself,
 it uses the output of the block,
 otherwise it looks up the power
 that the block receives from
 adjacent blocks. Left click with
 dye to change the LED color.
 Blinks with a frequency of about 0.5Hz.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_ent:
A small glass device that
 observes a defined volume and
 emits a redstone signal if it
 sees an entity. The volume is two
 blocks above and below, and 'range'
 in front, left and right (covers
 180 degrees). The range can be
 configured with small buttons on
 the front face, together with the
 output strength, the number of
 entities seen, and the entity type
 seen (mobs, players, animals, etc).
 Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on. Does not
 emit switching sounds.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.rustic_fa:
A metal trap door that swings
 open emits a redstone signal when
 something touches it. Output is
 to the block it is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.industria:
A display device that can be
 attached to a solid block to
 visualize the redstone power of
 that block. If the block can
 provide power itself, the gauge
 uses the output of it, otherwise
 the gauge will indirectly measure
 the power that the block receives
 from adjacent blocks. Strong and
 weak power is supported. Indirect
 weak measurements may have a
 short latency.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.oldfancy_:
A switch that outputs a redstone
 signal for a short time, like a
 button.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Activate while already active to
 extend the pulse time.
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Can be activated with projectiles.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.industria:
An industrial floor trap door
 that swings open and emits a
 redstone signal when someone or
 something falls on it. Output is
 to the block it is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).

A small device that observes a
 defined volume and emits a
 redstone signal if it sees an
 entity. The volume is two blocks
 above and below, and 'range' in
 front, left and right (covers
 180 degrees). The range can be
 configured with small buttons on
 the front face, together with the
 output strength, the number of
 entities seen, and the entity
 type seen (mobs, players, animals,
 etc). Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).

A relay-styled industrial control
 fitting for redstone panels. It
 behaves as a compact manual toggle.
 Use Switch Link receivers when
 you need remote pearl activation.

An industrial floor contact mat
 that emits a redstone signal when
 exposed to mechanical pressure on
 the top (e.g. someone standing on
 it), and a short time after the
 pressure was released. Outputs
 are to the front and bottom.
Click on the touch config up/down
 buttons to set output power,
 entity filter, entity count,
 and sensitivity.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).

Emits a signal with a defined on-
 and off-time. Configured using
 buttons on the front:
 - §1Blue: On-time§r
- §6Yellow: Off-time§r
- §2Green: Signal ramp up/down§r
- §4Red: Output power§r
 The timer is placed in standby
 mode for setup. Click on the front
 face to set it into run mode. Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
 As Switch Link target the timer
 toggles between run and standby
 mode.

A relay-styled control fitting for
 panel layouts. Use receivers and
 linked pearls for direct remote
 activation.

A small device that can be
 attached to a block to indicate
 if that block is powered. If the
 block can provide power itself,
 it uses the output of the block,
 otherwise it looks up the power
 that the block receives from
 adjacent blocks. Left click with
 dye to change the LED color.

 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).

 tint the switch.
